MINI MINUTES:

Russell Higgins, Esq., KNIGHT LAW GROUP, LLP (310) 552-2250 russellh@knightlaw.com Attorney for Plaintiffs, MARIANA GREUB and ESTON GREUB

Michael Folwy, Esq., BOWMAN AND BROOKE LLP 408/ 279-5393 efilehonda@bowmanandbrooke.com
Attorneys for Defendant AMERICAN HONDA MOTOR CO., INC.,

Hearing held. ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF MARIANA GREUB, ESTON GREUB's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatory 17.1, Set Two, From Defendant American Honda Motor Co., Inc.

The motion to compel further responses to form interrogatory 17.1, set two, is granted.

Plaintiff seeks discovery concerning documents she intends to use at trial; her discovery is targeted at preliminary facts that establish whether a business records hearsay exception is present. This is a permissible use of discovery. Defendant objects on grounds of relevance and contends that the Cadena litigation documents may not be used at trial. The court concludes that Plaintiff has made a threshold showing of relevance sufficient to satisfy Code of Civil Procedure, 2017.010. Defendant has not substantiated its burdens objections. (See West Pico Furniture Co. of Los Angeles v. Superior Court (1961) 56 Cal.2d 407, 417 ["The objection based upon burden must be sustained by evidence showing the quantum of work required, while to support an objection of oppression there must be some showing either of an intent to create an unreasonable burden or that the ultimate effect of the burden is incommensurate with the result sought."].)

Defendant shall provide further responses by March 4, 2026. No sanctions are awarded.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in copliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

Plaintiffs' motion to compel responses to requests for production of documents, set two, from defendant American Honda Motor Co. is denied.
